original_abstract:
This research explores the current landscape of military leadership through a descriptive study of the Armed Forces of Bosnia and Herzegovina. The primary objective is to evaluate the prevalence of specific leader characteristics, the nature of the military environment, the effectiveness of motivational strategies, and the perceived professional benefits of leadership experience within the army. Utilizing a survey-based methodology, data was collected from 200 active-duty commanders across various departments, including logistics, personnel management, and tactical support.

The descriptive analysis reveals a high level of agreement among respondents regarding the positive impact of military service on their managerial competence and leadership success. Participants characterized military leaders as being highly honest, decisive, and loyal to their organizations. Furthermore, the findings indicate that despite the inherent complexities of defense work, the internal military environment is perceived as trustworthy and ethical, characterized by routine activities and clear mission structures. In terms of leadership styles, the results highlight a predominant focus on task-oriented, team-based, and shared leadership models. Ultimately, the study suggests that military leadership training provides significant professional advantages and that commanders possess a high degree of confidence in their ability to motivate subordinates through established organizational hierarchies and specialized leadership skills.
humanized_abstract:
In the research entitled “Actual situation of military leadership in the Armed Forces of Bosnia and Herzegovina” there is a descriptive study of military leadership carried out in the Armed Forces of Bosnia and Herzegovina. The aim of the research is to determine the occurrence of defined leader characteristics in the Armed Forces of Bosnia and Herzegovina, as well as to establish the actual situation of the military environment, the motivation of soldiers and other benefits that military leaders gain through their duty. For the purposes of this research, a survey was used as a research method, where a sample of 200 active-duty commanders from various departments such as supply, personnel and support were taken into account.

On the basis of descriptive analysis, high level of consistency in opinions regarding the impact of military service on the management competencies and leadership success was achieved. The respondents characterised military leaders with high degree of honesty, professionalism and their loyalty to their organisation. The internal defence environment, which defence work belongs to, is a trustful and ethically acceptable environment, despite of a number of intricacies. The leadership styles which have been applied within defence sector are task-oriented, team-oriented and shared leadership. According to the results of the study, leadership training in the military significantly develops the professional capacities of military officers and that military commanders believe that they possess all the necessary skills to successfully motivate their troops via the military hierarchical structure and appropriate leadership skills.
